page: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 8 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% on stress-ng tests, while single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and Geekbench text processing score in the average range. However, multi-core performance is comparatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth tests also show weak performance across both small and large block read/write operations.

This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads that rely on steady single-threaded execution rather than intensive parallel processing or high memory throughput. It is appropriate for basic web serving, text processing, and lightweight database operations, though database benchmarks and static web serving throughput are relatively weak. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6a.xlarge offers a standard entry-level general-purpose resource profile, but users requiring high multi-threaded performance or local NVMe storage should consider alternative configurations.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 3.6 GHz, the instance is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, but multi-core execution and memory bandwidth fall into the lower percentiles. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, basic text processing, and lightweight web serving where single-threaded performance is prioritized over heavy parallel processing or high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, offering strong single-core but weaker multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, strong single-core performance, and standard DDR4 memory allocation.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Configured with 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR4 memory, and no local storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance but weaker multi-core execution
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for general-purpose applications, text processing, and light web serving
